title: Arguments to built-in functions should match documented types
description: Arguments to built-in functions should match documented typesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smelltype-dependentWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe types of the arguments that built-in functions accept are specified in the JavaScript language specification. Calls to these functions should conform to the documented types as they are designed to work with specific data types. If the arguments passed to these functions do not match the expected types, it can lead to type errors or unexpected behavior.Additionally, passing the correct types of arguments to built-in functions can improve performance by reducing the need for type conversions and other operations.const isTooSmall = Math.abs(x < 0.0042); // Noncompliant: 'Math.abs' takes a number as argumentEnsure that the
recommendation: Ensure arguments passed to built-in functions match their documented types.

noncompliant: const isTooSmall = Math.abs(x < 0.0042); // Noncompliant: 'Math.abs' takes a number as argument

checks.message: Built-in function arguments should match documented types
